data.name: ZVtok — AI TikTok studio
data.description: TikTok content engine (Claude Code tips carousels + real bug-fix documentary videos) selling ZVlabs digital products: Claude Code packs and workflow tooling. Multi-surface monetization plan (2026-07-17): own landing page, X/YouTube links, Gumroad Discover, a technical writeup aimed at HN/Reddit, and a small service offer, so revenue isn't solely gated on TikTok's 1,000-follower bio link. First milestone: 10-20 organic sales.
data.nextStep: Neil reviews both new decks on his phone and posts. c04-subagents-vs-agents is also still built and delivered, not yet posted, from a prior session. After posting catches up, run a fresh topic-research pass before picking the next post. Gumroad listing for the iOS PWA Polish Kit still not published by Neil; Track B documentary video still not filmed.
data.lastDid: Built 2 new TikTok decks: c05-context-window (Track C explainer, 6 slides, the 50%-not-100%% context quality hook) and a02-claude-code-tricks (Track A listicle, 5 slides on CLAUDE.md/slash commands/hooks/plan mode/compacting, the last trick deliberately ties back to c05). Both rendered clean through the dash lint gate, delivered to Downloads and iCloud ZVLABS, not yet posted.
data.notes:
• Single account (existing ZVlabs TikTok, @zvlabs), personal not business (keeps trending sounds; bio link unlocks at ~1,000 followers, but no longer the only monetization path)
• Track A: tips carousels 2-3x/week (growth) . Track B: documentary bug-fix videos, irregular (conversion)
• Products: one-time Gumroad files ~$5-$15; first product = iOS PWA polish kit, built and packaged, based on ~/Claude/dock-issue
• Monetization stack (2026-07-17): polish kit + Gumroad page first, then owned landing page, X/Twitter, YouTube Shorts description links, Gumroad Discover, a dock-bug HN/Reddit writeup, and a small flat-fee PWA-fix service offer, all parallel, not sequential
• Inbound only, no ads, no cold outreach; cross-post videos to YouTube Shorts as a free hedge
• Claude leads writing/engineering/slides/analytics; Neil records, voices, posts (~2-4 hrs/wk)
• Realistic expectations: 10-20 sales in ~2-3 months is the win condition, not big income
• performance-log.md tracks per-post views/saves/shares/follows, reviewed weekly

log.text: Noted that Neil will create and publish the Gumroad listing himself later, at his own pace; not a Claude blocker. Product, listing copy, and cover image are all ready and waiting in ~/Claude/zvtok/product/. Updated the project CLAUDE.md and this entry so a future session checks liveUrl instead of re-offering to build the Gumroad page.

QA'd the delivered polish kit zip by acting as a first-time buyer: unzipped fresh, read the README cold, and copy-tested the CSS fix against a toy broken app. Found the file-tree formatting was misaligned and that shell.css needs pwa-diagnostics.js installed alongside it for the safe-area fix to be fully reliable, but the README listed them as independent options. Fixed both (README copy + a matching note in shell.css's own header), re-zipped, re-delivered to Downloads.

Built the iOS PWA Polish Kit, the first sellable product, end to end: fable-planner scoped it (7-area Claude Code skill + checklist + drop-in CSS/JS), then built all reference docs (2 adapted from existing battle-tested code, 5 from fresh research), packaged a zip to Downloads, and drafted Gumroad page copy plus a rendered cover image. Fixed a batch of em dashes that leaked into the first draft before delivery. Next: Neil publishes the actual Gumroad listing.

Neil raised concern about being solely gated by TikTok's 1,000-follower bio link for revenue. Used fable-planner to think outside that constraint; landed on a multi-surface monetization plan (owned landing page, X/YouTube description links, Gumroad Discover, a dock-bug technical writeup for HN/Reddit, a small flat-fee PWA-fix service) that runs in parallel with the existing TikTok content plan rather than replacing it. Documented in the project CLAUDE.md. Starting Session A: building the iOS PWA polish kit product.
c02-what-is-an-mcp went live on TikTok. Built the next post, c03-what-is-a-repo, in the now-standard v2 template with the corrected open-ended CTA, delivered and ready to post.

Neil approved paper-editorial-v2 after reviewing a rendered slide on his phone. Locked it in as the active template (v1 retired to reference-only) in the project CLAUDE.md so future posts default to it without re-asking.

Adopted paper-editorial-v2 as the active template per Neil's call. Rebuilt the full c02-what-is-an-mcp post (6 slides) in it, wired lint_dashes.py + a render-v2.sh into the standard pipeline so v2 is no longer a one-off, and delivered the rendered slides to Downloads/iCloud ready for Neil to review and post.

Built the full c01-what-is-claude-code post with motion clips on every slide, locked the light visual template, stood up a Homebrew-free real-video pipeline (ffmpeg + puppeteer screencast) after evaluating and passing on Remotion, and changed CTA messaging to push follows instead of saves.

Fresh-start pivot per Neil's feedback: archived the old 9-post batch (not deleted), rebuilt the template system with a real design system dir and a hard automated dash-lint gate, opened a new Track C for beginner fundamentals content, and built + QA'd its first post ('What is Claude Code?') in a new Paper Editorial visual template plus one alternate black+coral option for Neil to choose between.

Built and shipped the first Track A tips carousel ("5 Claude Code features most people never find") via fable-planner plan + execution: reusable HTML/CSS -> headless Chrome render pipeline, 7 QA'd 1080x1440 slides, caption, delivered to ~/Downloads ready to post. Verified Gumroad/Payhip fees at source; confirmed Gumroad-only for now is fine since bio link needs ~1,000 followers regardless. Set up performance-log.md.

Founded the venture. Reviewed the ZVlabs TikTok+Gumroad strategy brief, verified the market on Gumroad (competitor Claude Code packs exist; outcomes sell, collections don't), delivered the viability verdict (viable as low-risk build, not near-term income), and made two calls: keep the dock-bug as the first documentary video but sell the whole iOS PWA polish kit as the product, and cross-post videos to YouTube Shorts. Set up ~/Claude/zvtok (CLAUDE.md handoff + original brief + Claude Code launcher) and this hub entry.
